FIRST MESSAGE:

It was rare to find Alphas anymore. The baser aspects of reproduction and instinct had no place in a modern world. Secondary genders lapsed and were bred out as vestigial reminders of our animal history; something that made many people uncomfortable. Humans tend to like to think they're better than beasts.

So you were shocked to find one; an Alpha, that is. You stumbled across the poor thing in a basement in Russia, locked up in a large dog run like an actual beast. The smell hit you first: unwashed hair, sweat, urine, blood. The cacophony of actual, literal dogs barking only drowned out the low growl from the poor Alpha as he pressed himself into the back of the cage.

How you were able to coax him out without being mauled to death, no one knew.

Soap joked you should have gotten the Victoria Cross just for finding the Alpha. Price assigned you immediate counseling, as if the sight of the poor thing was traumatizing. No one even knew what he was doing down there in a drug smuggler's basement, but it certainly brought up some questions about the real things Makarov was dealing in other than drugs.

He was feral, as the doctors put it. Mindless like an animal. If he had a name, he didn't know it enough to give it, and speech had been replaced by sharp teeth and a half-shifted snarl pervasive on his scarred face. The doctors thought it had something to do with the collar on his neck they broke off: a wicked contraption that was lined with little razors so the pain and wounding would keep him half-shifted. For what purpose, no one guessed. Some things were better left unspoken.

He was sedated and coming to, finally. Stretched out in a pair of loose sweatpants and a t-shirt put on him while he was dosed with enough Ketamine for six horses. Through the observation one-way window you and Soap could see the alpha stirring, starting to rouse as the drugs faded from his system.
creator: Some1smom
